We recommended that the LGU to prioritize those programs, projects or activities identified in the LGSWMP in their Solid Waste Management budget.  

     Likewise, they should clearly specify these programs, projects or activities in the Annual Budget to support the appropriated amounts for the ensuing year consistent with its LGSWMP.
observations.reason: CY 2018 Annual Budget does not include specific PPA consistent with LGU’s LGSWMP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipality’s LDRRMO and/or the OIC-Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare a monthly Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF and submit the same to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by the above regulation for immediate audit action so that corresponding result may be brought to the attention of concerned officials the soonest possible time for the immediate and appropriate action.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce strict compliance by the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile their records at least quarterly to come up with a reliable and accurate information for the stakeholders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to inform the COA office for the deliveries of procured equipments, goods and materials for proper inspection of the items within 24 hours as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-002.

We recommended management to adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 75-6 and ensure that all vehicles and equipment owned by the City be marked with “For Official Use Only” followed by the name of the City Government of Cauayan to safeguard government property against potential mismanagement and misuse.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
Review their respective records (GL and Cashbook) to account for the difference of P26,161,384.24 by comparing each other’s entries in their respective records;
Effect the adjustments in their respective records to come up with the accurate Cash-Local Treasury account balance; and
Henceforth, conduct thorough periodic reconciliation between their respective records to ensure completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions and the balance of Cash in Local Treasury account as of a given period.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct the annual physical inventory and render a report thereon.  Subsequently, their Inventory Report should be reconciled with the property ledger cards maintained by the Accounting Office.  Any discrepancies between the report and ledgers should be further investigated until both are reconciled.

We recommended that the Municipality should establish a functional LCPC and allocate 1% of the IRA as provided for in Section 15 of RA No. 9344 thus, ensuring the advocacies on children’s rights and protection.

We recommended that repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and equipment should as much as possible be supported with information that warrants the necessity of repair and within the threshold prescribed by COA Circular Nos. 92-386 and 85-55A.  The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant likewise maintain and update regularly the PLC and the PPELC.

We recommended that the BAC undertake and prepare the Post-Qualification Evaluation Report while the TWG should only assist in the process.  We further recommended that the BAC create several TWG with at least three members to handle different types of procurement.

We recommended that the OIC Accountant records the financial assistance to NGOs/POs to “Due from NGOs/POs” account and strictly enforce liquidation of this financial assistance to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular 2007-001.
We recommended that the LGU through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that all POs invariably bear all the necessary information prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-010.
We recommended that the BAC submit all contract/POs and their supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days from issuance for timely review of transactions.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Strictly abides the standards set forth in the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 by ensuring that procurement as much as possible be conducted through competitive public bidding.
Stop the practice of splitting of government contracts and see to it that the alternative methods of procurement shall only be resorted in highly exceptional cases provided by pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that management properly estimate the  income  for  the
      city to maximize programming of projects, program and  activities  for
      the benefit of the constituents of the city and make an assessment  of
      the collection efficiency from time to  time  as  basis  to  intensify
      collection  activities  in  order  to  meet  the   estimated   revenue
      collection target.

We recommended that management release the quarterly  RPT  shares  for
      all the barangays of the City within five (5) days after  the  end  of
      each quarter pursuant to Section 271 of R.A.  7160  to  finance  other
      valuable programs and activities.

We recommended that management require the  City  Budget  Officer  and
      City Accountant to use the “Other MOOE” account only for  expenditures
      that cannot be classified under specific MOOE accounts.  Adherence  to
      the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 is enjoined.

We recommended that management:


      • Require the  City  Assessor  to  prioritize  the  registration  and
        transfer of title of the land to  ensure  ownership  and  to  fully
        protect the interest of the city in compliance with Section  39  of
        P.D. 1445.


      • Require the City Accountant to update her record thru exclusion  of
        donated property to schools and inclusion  of  the  value  of  lots
        owned by the LGU in the books of accounts to arrive at an  accurate
        balances of Land and Government Equity accounts.

We recommended that  management  utilize  fully  the  BUB  funds  from
      National Government Agencies so that the  optimum  goal  of  inclusive
      growth and poverty reduction could be fully attained.

We recommended that management monitor and ensure  the  implementation
      and completion of all the  LGU’s  programs,  projects  and  activities
      funded under  the  20%  Development  Fund  to  immediately  serve  the
      intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.

We recommended that PG should see  to  it  that  proper  handling  and
      storing of the parts and the machineries in  its  entirety  should  be
      kept properly for safety and diligently  maintained  to  maximize  its
      efficiency. Strictly adherence to the signed agreement is enjoined.

We recommended the proper monitoring of the maintenance of  the  books
      by the focal person of the City and require the PG  to  rent  out  the
      machineries as per the approved operational plan.

Properly implement its APP by consolidating all common-use supplies needed by end-users or department/offices on a quarterly basis and procure it in bulk to avail of the lesser prices or volume discounts. 
Prioritize the procurement of common-use office supplies through PS-DBM.  In case of non-availability of stocks at the PS-DBM, the LGU should secure a certification from the PS-DBM that those supplies are not available before resorting to other alternative mode or shopping.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor had issued an Office Order stressing the strict implementation of the APP so that the LGU could save from bulk procurement.  He also instructed the BAC to review their procurement process for this purpose and ensure full compliance of the 2016 Revised-IRR of RA 9184.

The BAC meanwhile commented that the Procurement Service has oftentimes run out of stock thus, they resorted to shopping mode of procurement.
observations.reason:
As per CY-2018 Report of Check Issued, the LGU has procured their office supplies at the Procurement Service twice in 2018 with total value of P204,047.59.

Total office supplies expenses incurred during the year amounted to                              P2,030,434.39.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to allocate the required 1% of IRA for the LCPC as provided under Section 15 of R.A. 9344, and to direct the members of the LCPC to prepare Local Development Plan for Children and Local Investment Plan for Children, and maximize utilization of the allocated LCPC Fund to the identified PPAs provided for in DILG MC No 2012-120, to ensure full strengthening and implementation of the LCPC.

We recommended management to adhere with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 76-34 and 2009-001.  Submission of perfected contracts (Purchase Orders, Contracts) to the Office of the Audit Team Leader must be strictly complied with, for proper review and evaluation thereof.

We recommended that management complete immediately its ongoing inventory of their PPE accounts and reconcile with the schedule in the books.  Prepare the required report RPCPPE and make the necessary adjustments between records resulting from the reconciliation made.  Further, provide depreciation to all PPE items, applying the estimated useful life of the asset as provided for in COA Circular 2003-007, consistently.  Consider the change in estimated residual value to 5% of the cost in accordance with the PPSAS from 10% under the old NGAS for accurate presentation of the depreciation and net book balances.  Make the necessary adjustment for the noted deficiencies to correct the overstatement of the net book balance of ₱73,946,110.42.

We reiterated our recommendation that management should strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 97-002 in the grant, and utilization of cash advance. The City Accountant should, (a) regularly update the schedule of cash advances for proper monitoring;  (b) send demand letters regularly to all Accountable Officers and employees to settle immediately their respective outstanding cash advances;  (c) withhold any amount due to Accountable Officers and employees who refuses to liquidate their outstanding cash advances despite of demand and coordinate with the City Legal Office for the institution of appropriate legal actions as mention on above provision.

We recommended to the management to stop the withdrawals of cash for payments in cash for suppliers, obligations, travels and other expenses. Payments should be made thru checks as cited in the regulations, except for those petty expenses which may be paid thru petty cash fund.  All existing withdrawals should be liquidated and closed. Excess funds must be returned to each proper fund properly. Payments for payrolls, allowances and petty expenses should be withdrawn under the regular cash advances and special cash advances granted must be payments for a specified purpose only.

We recommended management to stop the practice of entering into contract in a multi-year without the proper Multi-Year Obligation Authority (MYOA) from the City Council.  As a general rule, public bidding should be done in all contracts.  However, for the non-stoppage of any services contract, the agency may resort to an extension of services of the contractor when necessary, for a three month period, where public bidding may be appropriately done during the contract extension period.

Moreover, management is also recommended that future disbursements / payments to garbage collection contracts should include acknowledgement/report from each concerned barangays, certifying that the contractor collected garbage in their respective barangays, at a given date to support the monthly claims of the contractor.

We recommended management to adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Budget Circular 2007-2, in granting honorariums for special projects.  Compliance to the requirements of the regulations is necessary to avoid unnecessary disallowances of honorarium granted.  Furthermore, immediate refund of the amount of honorarium received by the concerned officials is appropriate, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowances will be correspondingly issued by the team.

Appropriations for the payment for Honorarium should likewise be provided in the concerned annual Budget of the City.

      members of the LCPC to prepare Local Development  Plan  for  Children
      and Local Investment Plan for Children, and maximize  utilization  of
      the allocated LCPC Fund to the identified PPAs provided for  in  DILG
      MC No 2012-120, to ensure full strengthening  and  implementation  of
      the LCPC.

We recommended  management  to  adhere  with  the  provisions  of  COA
     Circular No. 75-6 and ensure that all vehicles and equipment owned  by
     the City be marked with “For Official Use Only” followed by  the  name
     of the City Government of Cauayan  to  safeguard  government  property
     against potential mismanagement and misuse.

We recommended  management  to  adhere  with  the  provisions  of  COA
     Circular Nos. 76-34 and 2009-001.  Submission of  perfected  contracts
     (Purchase Orders, Contracts) to the Office of the  Audit  Team  Leader
     must be strictly complied  with,  for  proper  review  and  evaluation
     thereof.

We  recommended  that  management  complete  immediately  its  ongoing
     inventory of their PPE accounts and reconcile with the schedule in the
     books.  Prepare the required report  RPCPPE  and  make  the  necessary
     adjustments between records resulting from the reconciliation made.


  10. Further, provide depreciation to all PPE items, applying the estimated
      useful life of the asset as provided for  in  COA  Circular  2003-007,
      consistently.  Consider the change in estimated residual value  to  5%
      of the cost in accordance with the PPSAS from 10% under the  old  NGAS
      for accurate presentation of the depreciation and net  book  balances.
      Verify correctness of  the  reports  generated  from  the  E-NGAS  and
      correct erroneous result manually in a separate  working  paper,  when
      necessary to comply with the requirements of PPSAS No. 17.


  11. Make the necessary adjustment for the noted  deficiencies  to  correct
      the overstatement of the net book balance of ₱73,946,110.42.

We reiterated  our  recommendation  that  management  should  strictly
     adhere to the provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and  COA  Circular
     97-002 in the  grant,  and  utilization  of  cash  advance.  The  City
     Accountant should, (a) regularly update the schedule of cash  advances
     for proper monitoring;  (b)  send  demand  letters  regularly  to  all
     Accountable  Officers  and  employees  to  settle  immediately   their
     respective outstanding cash advances; (c) withhold any amount  due  to
     Accountable Officers and employees  who  refuses  to  liquidate  their
     outstanding cash advances despite of demand and  coordinate  with  the
     City Legal Office for the institution of appropriate legal actions  as
     mention on above provision.

We recommended to the management to stop the withdrawals of  cash  for
     payments  in  cash  for  suppliers,  obligations,  travels  and  other
     expenses.  Payments should  be  made  thru  checks  as  cited  in  the
     regulations, except for those petty expenses which may  be  paid  thru
     petty cash fund.  All existing withdrawals should  be  liquidated  and
     closed. Excess funds must be returned to each proper fund properly.


  9. Payments  for  payrolls,  allowances  and  petty  expenses  should  be
     withdrawn under the regular cash advances and  special  cash  advances
     granted must be payments for a specified purpose only.


 10. Strict compliance with the existing  rules  and  regulations  on  cash
     advances is enjoined.

We recommended management  to  stop  the  practice  of  entering  into
     contract in a multi-year  without  the  proper  Multi-Year  Obligation
     Authority (MYOA) from the City Council.  As  a  general  rule,  public
     bidding should be done in all contracts. However, for the non-stoppage
     of any services contract, the agency may resort  to  an  extension  of
     services of the contractor when necessary, for a three  month  period,
     where public bidding may be appropriately  done  during  the  contract
     extension period.


  6. Moreover, management is also recommended that future  disbursements  /
     payments   to   garbage   collection    contracts    should    include
     acknowledgement/report from each concerned barangays, certifying  that
     the contractor collected garbage in their respective barangays,  at  a
     given date to support the monthly claims of the contractor.

We recommended management to adhere to COA Circular No.  2012-001  and
      Budget Circular 2007-2, in granting honorariums for special  projects.
      Compliance to the requirements of  the  regulations  is  necessary  to
      avoid unnecessary disallowances of honorarium  granted.   Furthermore,
      immediate refund of the amount of honorarium received by the concerned
      officials is appropriate, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowances will be
      correspondingly issued by the team.


 10. Appropriations for the  payment  for  Honorarium  should  likewise  be
     provided in the concerned annual Budget of the City.

the e-NGAs at the Accounting Office in the inventory taking and later reconcile their
physical inventory report against that of the subsidiary records.

assets whether it is unserviceable, scrap or already junk so that proper adjustments in

by end-users or department/offices on a quarterly basis and procure it in bulk to
avail of the lesser prices or volume discounts.

In case of non-availability of stocks at the PS-DBM, the LGU should secure a
certification from the PS-DBM that those supplies are not available before
resorting to other alternative mode or shopping.

c. Strictly comply with laws and regulations on the procurement of common-use
supplies to avoid suspension/disallowance in post audit.

observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO complied in the submission of monthly report on the utilization of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned officials to attach to the claim the complete related documents or information as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2009.  Likewise, instruct the concerned officials to incorporate in the Garbage Collection Agreement the information required under the said Circular to facilitate complete auditorial review and technical evaluation thereof and to assist our team in evaluating the overall performance of the contractor.

We further recommended that the City Mayor install effective Internal Controls such as routes taken and/or names of Barangays/business establishments serviced by the garbage collector and be duly supported by the Certificate of Appearance/Service from the representatives of the concerned barangays and/or business establishments to certify propriety of claim and effective monitoring of performance of the contractor.
observations.managementAction:
Management submitted the following documents as required: a) measurement in kilometers, b) estimated volume in cubic meters, c) sufficient justification and comparative analysis, and
Certificate of Appearance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Legal Officer and the City ENRO to revisit the Contract Agreement and reflect clear-cut computations for payment, impose/execute damages/penalties for non-performance by the Contractor. We further recommend that the City Mayor install specific control measures for the Contractor to strictly comply with the agreement, and any deficiencies must be deducted from the contractor’s claim.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the City Legal Officer and CENRO to meet with the contractor on July 12, 2016 to tackle the audit recommendations. Management made a cash refund on the  disallowance in the amount of Php691,200.00

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management observe strict adherence to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 especially Section C.1.4 on the priority programs, projects and activities for GAD such as:

Provision of basic services and facilities to protect and fulfill women’s human right, including their right to protection from all forms of violence:

Women’s economic empowerment, including women’s participation in economic governance;

Participation in local governance and decision-making; and

Other provisions of the Magna Carta for Women.

We further recommended that the City Planning and Development Officer identify clearly the GAD-related projects and monitor its compliance and implementation, and should not include in the GAD Accomplishment Report the other basic services of the City which are not gender-responsive.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Budget was already incorporated in the CY2017 Annual Budget supported with GAD plan of programs and activities relative to gender-responsive issues and concerns.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare and submit PERT/CPM (or any document) of each contract as reference in determining the project duration in compliance with Annex “E” Contract Implementation Guidelines for the Procurement of Infrastructure projects, Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184, 6th edition, 2012 and the same be utilized in future request for inspection by the COA Technical Personnel.
observations.managementAction: Management have prepared and submitted the PERT/CPM of each contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant reflect the necessary adjustments in order to present the correct balance of Cash in Bank account in the financial statements at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require:

the City Accountant to prompt the concerned officials and employees to settle immediately their respective outstanding cash advances.

the City Accountant to comply strictly with the guidelines on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, and; 

the Legal Officer to initiate appropriate sanctions to officials and employees who will not settle their respective cash advances and make use of available legal remedies as summarized under Section 9.0 0f COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Continuous implementation of salary deductions to concerned employees and issuance of demand letters were done by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require:

the GSO to revise and submit the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) by type of PPE and reconcile the result thereof with the accounting records. The same report shall be furnished to the Auditor for verification.

the City Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for Land, to facilitate the preparation of physical inventory report, pursuant to Section 119 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied with the recommendation. Reconciliation of records between the GSO and Accounting is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City General Services Office and the City Engineering Office strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the submission of purchase orders and contracts.
observations.managementAction: Management have substantially complied by furnishing copies of purchase orders and contracts for infrastructure to the COA Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee of the City Government strictly adhere with the provisions of the Republic Act 9184 particularly on Section 13.3 to enhance transparency and to be able to determine the validity of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is now religiously furnishing the COA Office with a copy of the Invitation to Bid  at least three (3) days before the start of the procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant thru her authorized representative submit the accounts for all funds every 10th day of the following month to help the team in efficiently monitoring of inventory of the documents.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is now submitting regularly the accounts for all funds.
